chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْحَجِّ مَاشِياً وَ انْقِطَاعِ مَشْيِ الْمَاشِي
chapter_title.en: Chapter 158 – The going to the Hajj walking and the cutting off the walk of the walker

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/110)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/110)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْمَشْيُ فِي الْحَجِّ إِذَا رَمَى الْجِمَارَ زَارَ الْبَيْتَ رَاكِباً وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ismail Bin Hammam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Regarding the one upon whom is (a vow for) the walking during the Hajj, when he pelts the rocks, can visit the House (Kabah) riding, and there would be nothing upon him’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Isma’il ibn Hammam who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the one who travels on foot for al-Hajj, has said, ‘When one throws pebbles on Jamar, he can visit the House riding; and he is not obligated for anything in this matter.’”
